name: IBM Bob × ContextForge — control plane demo
//: Opens this repo in a GitHub Codespace with Docker + toolchain preinstalled, so the WHOLE governed mesh runs in the cloud. Attendees then install ONLY IBM Bob locally and point it at the forwarded :4444 gateway (see 'make connect'). No local Docker/uv/make needed.
//features: sshd lets `gh codespace ssh` exec/forward into the box (the base image ships no SSH server); needed to drive/verify the mesh from a laptop CLI.
//hostRequirements: A MINIMUM floor. The mesh runs in ~2-3GB at rest. Set to 2-core/8gb so the cheapest (free-tier-friendly) Codespaces machine is AVAILABLE and the local Dev Containers extension doesn't warn on <16GB Docker. For a faster first build, just pick the 4-core/16gb machine in the Codespaces 'change machine type' menu — this floor doesn't cap it.
//forwardPorts: 6277 is the MCP Inspector's PROXY — its UI (6274) runs in your laptop browser and talks to the proxy, so 6277 must be forwarded AND set Public or `make inspect-mcp` fails with 'Error Connecting to MCP Inspector Proxy'. (GitHub re-forwards as Private each launch; flip 6277 → Public in the PORTS tab. CLI check needs neither: `make inspect-tools`.)

4444.label: ContextForge gateway — point Bob here (make this Public)

6277.label: MCP Inspector — PROXY (make Public for browser Connect)
